repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When generating operator prototxt for an operator already defined via REG_OP in autogen/op_proto.h, do not include a duplicate operator definition using OpDef and OP_ADD; instead rely solely on the REG_OP macro.
When fixing a redefinition error of REG_OP(Ca6k04592Custom) in op_proto.h during build, do not delete the entire OpDef class and OP_ADD call; instead remove one of

When opbuild fails with redefinition of `class ge::op::Ca6k00322Custom` in `autogen/op_proto.h`, do not add a duplicate `REG_OP` block in op host source files; instead remove any extra `REG_OP` definition and rely on the single definition in the auto-generated header.

When build fails in 02-build due to redefinition of 'Ca6k00045Custom' in op_proto.h caused by manual OP_ADD in host_tiling_src while autogen REG_OP already defines the operator, do not add a second registration with OP_ADD; instead remove the manual OP_ADD and rely solely on the autogen REG_OP.

repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When opbuild fails with redefinition of 'class ge::op::Ca6k00285Custom' from REG_OP in op_proto.h, do not include duplicate REG_OP(Ca6k00285Custom) macro invocations; instead ensure the operator is defined exactly once (e.g., by generating a single REG_OP invocation in the autogen header).

repair_memory_selection.selection_rationale: All five memories address the exact failure pattern: redefinition of ‘class ge::op::Ca6k...Custom’ in autogen/op_proto.h due to duplicate REG_OP or conflicting OpDef+REG_OP. The current failure is for ca6k_04671, showing redefinition plus DuplicateSectionError in the ops-info.ini. Each memory advises removing the duplicate operator registration (e.g., delete the second REG_OP, keep only one definition, or remove OpDef when REG_OP already exists). They are directly applicable because they come from the same torch#1 category and no_tool mode, and cover operators ca6k_04615, ca6k_04592, ca6k_00322, ca6k_00045, ca6k_00285 – all with the same underlying cause.
